acceptance_targets: [CH18_CRAFT_ADDICTION_TIMIDITY] In the revised post-group span beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:123 and ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:139, a reader can point to at least two concrete present-day manifestations of Margot's craving (physical symptom, compulsive behavior, or restraint cost) before she goes back to the facility. If the section still ends only on an abstract statement of wanting without those present-tense markers, this finding remains unresolved.

[CH18_CRAFT_SYMMETRICAL_AFTERMATH] After revision, no more than one of the three POV sections may end by abstractly summarizing its own meaning. The other section endings must land on distinct observable turns anchored to the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23-29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:71-75, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:137-139 while still keeping the chapter's state shift 'not resolution but a new compromised normal' legible.

[CH18_YUSUF_ARC_OFFPAGE] Pass if Chapter 18 contains a Yusuf POV section of at least 40 lines that dramatizes the publication through scene action (not summary) and includes at least one interaction that tests the consequences of going public. Fail if the publication remains mediated entirely through another character's phone or is rendered only in summary narration.

[CH18_KAI_EPILOGUE_INERT] Pass if Kai's Ch 18 section contains at least one scene with dialogue where his post-Doha state is tested or revealed through interaction with another person. Fail if the section remains entirely interior narration with no dialogue and no external test.

[SOMATIC_CH18] Pass if the wrist references in Kai's Ch 18 section total no more than two brief clauses and neither re-explains the healing baseline or uses 'sealed too fast.' Fail if any Ch 18 wrist passage re-teaches the too-fast healing or stacks multiple baseline descriptors without new meaning.
[length_chapter_18_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised post-group span beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:123 and ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:139, a reader can point to at least two concrete present-day manifestations of Margot's craving (physical symptom, compulsive behavior, or restraint cost) before she goes back to the facility. If the section still ends only on an abstract statement of wanting without those present-tense markers, this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: Margot's post-therapy aftermath names the addiction but then lets the present-tense craving go oddly bloodless. After the scene establishes neural-interface addiction as the chapter's hardest material, the prose shifts into a pretty Munich walk and a tasteful surfer study; the current cost of wanting is mostly abstracted into 'the wanting was there' instead of being rendered through bodily compulsion or behavior on the page. That reads as timidity about the ongoing addiction reality rather than fully inhabited aftermath.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the post-group movement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:125, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:133, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:139 so the Eisbach walk still exists but the present craving acquires concrete physical or behavioral form. Options: let Stefan's withdrawal echo in Margot's own body, let the surfers trigger a compulsive scan for feed-like access, or let the return to the facility include a visible self-management cost. Keep the ordinary-sameness idea, but make the sameness legible through action rather than only summary language.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, no more than one of the three POV sections may end by abstractly summarizing its own meaning. The other section endings must land on distinct observable turns anchored to the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23-29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:71-75, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:137-139 while still keeping the chapter's state shift 'not resolution but a new compromised normal' legible.
findings.problem: The three aftermath movements keep arriving at the same calibrated conclusion in slightly different words: things continue, it is enough, the time is hers, the wanting is ordinary and entire. Because each section closes by paraphrasing the chapter's thesis instead of buying a distinct local turn, the finale starts to feel architected around symmetrical emotional beats rather than discovered through separate character pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revisit the section exits at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:75, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:139. Preserve the required compromised-normal state, but let at least two of the three sections end on a different kind of dramatic evidence: a concrete choice deferred with cost, an observable movement toward or away from another person, a public consequence entering the body, or a relapse-adjacent action. Avoid closing every vignette by restating its meaning in abstract summary terms.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Chapter 18 contains a Yusuf POV section of at least 40 lines that dramatizes the publication through scene action (not summary) and includes at least one interaction that tests the consequences of going public. Fail if the publication remains mediated entirely through another character's phone or is rendered only in summary narration.

evidence.excerpt: 3652: In the changing room, Lena sat on the bench in her suit, dripping, and read the article on her phone.

3654: Yusuf had published the analysis three days ago on ResearchGate. The title was long and flat in academic engineering: "Structural Analysis of Firmware Corruption Patterns in Shared Bio-Integrative Calibration Networks: Correspondence with Proprietary Development Tool Signatures." Under it sat two public repositories: the virus-analysis files, and a de-identified extract from the athlete biometrics in Yusuf's archive. His name was on it. Kirchberg was listed as his former affiliation.

3666: Whether it would help Farah, Lena didn't know. She didn't know enough about Voss-Kessler to pretend otherwise. But Yusuf had written *Farah says hi* as if it were ordinary. His sister was still there, still speaking into the future, and the work he had risked everything for had made it into public hands. It wasn't a victory. It was enough.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
3652: In the changing room, Lena sat on the bench in her suit, dripping, and read the article on her phone.
3653: 
3654: Yusuf had published the analysis three days ago on ResearchGate. The title was long and flat in academic engineering: "Structural Analysis of Firmware Corruption Patterns in Shared Bio-Integrative Calibration Networks: Correspondence with Proprietary Development Tool Signatures." Under it sat two public repositories: the virus-analysis files, and a de-identified extract from the athlete biometrics in Yusuf's archive. His name was on it. Kirchberg was listed as his former affiliation.
3655: 
3656: The article had been live for three days and Meridian's stock had dropped eleven percent. Lena had read that in the business section of the NZZ after opening the link. Meridian had issued a statement denying the findings and announcing an internal review. The Swiss Federal Office of Sport had opened a preliminary inquiry. The European Athletics Enhancement Authority had requested the files everyone else could already download.
3657: 
[...]
3661: 
3662: His reply came four minutes later: *Yeah. Farah says hi.*
3663: 
3664: Lena sat with the phone in her hand. The changing...

findings.problem: Yusuf's decision to publish the Meridian analysis -- the culmination of his entire arc (data theft in Ch 3, alternative patch in Ch 9, confrontation with Kai in Ch 14) -- is resolved entirely off-page. Lena reads the ResearchGate article on her phone in a changing room. The publication's consequences (11% stock drop, federal inquiry, Farah's data going public) are summarized in narration. Yusuf gets no POV scene in the novel's final chapter despite having full POV chapters earlier (Ch 3, Ch 9). His texted reply ('Yeah. Farah says hi.') is the only direct speech from a character whose defining action is happening right now. This is the novel's most consequential compression failure: a four-chapter arc culminates in someone else's phone notification.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a Yusuf POV section to Chapter 18, positioned before or after Lena's swimming section. Target the space currently occupied by snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3652-3666 -- either replace Lena's mediated summary with a Yusuf scene, or insert a new section break before line 3627 with Yusuf's perspective. The scene should dramatize the act and cost of going public: the moment of posting, a call with Farah that carries both hope and uncertainty, and at least one piece of immediate fallout (a message from Petra, a notification from Meridian, a colleague's reaction). The scene should render Yusuf's own awareness that the published data may or may not help Farah -- the uncertainty is the point.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Kai's Ch 18 section contains at least one scene with dialogue where his post-Doha state is tested or revealed through interaction with another person. Fail if the section remains entirely interior narration with no dialogue and no external test.

evidence.excerpt: 3608: His right hand lifted the phone before he meant it to, and he set it back on the table.

3614: He thought about calling Lena. Lena was in Montreux, thirty minutes by train, swimming at a municipal center that charged her forty francs a month for a lane and did not ask about the port at the base of her skull. They saw each other two or three times a week. It wasn't dramatic. They cooked, or she cooked and put him to work chopping onions because she had no patience for anyone standing in her kitchen being decorative. They talked, or didn't. Once, in her bed, his hand had slid up the inside of her thigh and then closed too hard, thumb digging in before he knew he'd done it. He had felt the grip lock straight into his forearm. Lena had sucked in air, caught his wrist with both hands, and held it there until the pressure went out. Then she'd turned his palm over, set it flat on her stomach, and pulled him back down. He could still feel that pause now: heat in his face, the delayed release in his fingers, her skin warm under his hand after.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
3597: 
3598: Kai sat at an outside table at a cafe on the Rue du Grand-Pont, his coffee half-finished and the afternoon showing no interest in moving. Lausanne in August: the lake below the old town catching light between buildings, tourists on the steep sidewalks with shopping bags and good shoes, a woman at the next table reading something on a tablet with the absolute attention of someone who did not want to be disturbed. The cathedral's tower was visible above the rooftops, grey stone against a sky that had been blue all day and looked ready to stay that way until evening.
3599: 
3600: His wrist ached, the same deep tight ache that arrived every afternoon where the break had sealed too fast. He rotated it once and the ache stayed.
3601: 
3602: The email from SportsVision was still open on his phone, sitting face-down next to the coffee. It was the third time they'd reached out. They wanted Kai Brennan, dead last in the 200 meters in Doha, on the commentary team for the next Apex Games cycle, explaining the sport to people who liked the spectacle more than the body inside it. He'd be useful now as the athlete who failed in public and kept talking afterward.
[...]
3620: A pigeon...

findings.problem: The protagonist's epilogue (lines 3597-3625, approximately 28 lines) consists entirely of undramatized interiority: Kai sits at a cafe, his wrist aches, he thinks about a commentary job offer, his hand lifts his phone before he means to, he doesn't call Lena, a pigeon lands. There is no scene, no dialogue, no interaction, and no moment where the state established in Doha (the man who stopped his own race) is tested by the world. The hand-lifting-the-phone detail (line 3608) is the one beat with genuine craft resonance, but it passes without scene consequence. Compare Margot's section in the same chapter (lines 3672-3734), which delivers a full therapy scene and a full observation scene. Kai's section reads as if the novel has exhausted its material for its central character.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand Kai's section at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3597-3625 to include at least one scene with external interaction. Options: (a) the SportsVision producer meets Kai in Lausanne and the conversation tests whether Kai can articulate what happened in Doha without accepting the 'equipment trouble' framing; (b) a stranger recognizes him from the race and says something that forces Kai to decide in real time what version to give; (c) a scene with Lena where the daily version of the system-ahead-of-him creates friction or tenderness in an ordinary moment. The section needs at least one interaction where another person's presence externalizes Kai's state.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the wrist references in Kai's Ch 18 section total no more than two brief clauses and neither re-explains the healing baseline or uses 'sealed too fast.' Fail if any Ch 18 wrist passage re-teaches the too-fast healing or stacks multiple baseline descriptors without new meaning.

evidence.excerpt: 3600: His wrist ached, the same deep tight ache that arrived every afternoon where the break had sealed too fast. He rotated it once and the ache stayed.

3618: The waiter set down the cup. Kai thanked him. Warmth spread through both palms, the left still wrong deep in the bone, the right carrying that familiar seam under the skin. He drank. His hand set the cup back on the saucer with the same unwanted steadiness, and he watched a boat on the lake through the gap between buildings. The offer stayed undecided. The wrist kept aching. The afternoon kept going.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 3600: His wrist ached, the same deep tight ache that arrived every afternoon where the break had sealed too fast. He rotated it once and the ache stayed.

findings.problem: 'the same deep tight ache that arrived every afternoon where the break had sealed too fast' (3600) re-teaches the healing baseline in the novel's final pages. Line 3618 stacks two baseline descriptors in one sentence: 'the left still wrong deep in the bone, the right carrying that familiar seam under the skin.' In the epilogue, the wrist should carry accumulated meaning -- what it now means to live with a body that healed wrong -- not replay the explanatory baseline.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3600, cut 'the same deep tight ache that arrived every afternoon where the break had sealed too fast' and replace with a single sensory beat that shows how Kai now lives with the wrist rather than re-explaining its origin. E.g., 'His wrist found the weather before he did' or simply 'The wrist.' At line 3618, keep at most one of the two descriptors and let the other land as compressed gesture. The novel's final wrist reference should feel like arrival, not reminder.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
